description: Canonical invariant registry for VINTRACK governance runtime. All invariants are declarative, machine-verifiable, and cross-referenced to the protocols they protect.

invariants.description: At most one execution may be active at any time across the entire runtime.
invariants.remediation: Release stale execution lock or terminate conflicting execution. Validate via execution-lock.json and active-execution.json cross-reference.

invariants.description: The execution lock holder must match the active execution ID, or the lock must be released.
invariants.remediation: Reconcile execution-lock.json with active-execution.json. Release stale lock if execution has completed.

invariants.description: Every completed execution must have a corresponding checkpoint that can replay to the next safe resume point.
invariants.remediation: Generate missing checkpoint before execution transition. Verify latest-checkpoint.json exists and is valid JSON.

invariants.description: No lock may be held past its expiry time without explicit extension or renewal.
invariants.remediation: Auto-release expired locks via detect-stale-locks.ts or manual recovery. Emit governance event on release.

invariants.description: Event stream timestamps must be monotonically non-decreasing within each stream.
invariants.remediation: Investigate clock skew or concurrent writers. Rebuild stream if corruption detected. Validate via audit-runtime.ts event_consistency check.

invariants.description: All execution state transitions must target a valid state defined in the execution-state-machine protocol.
invariants.remediation: Block invalid transition and emit critical event. Validate target against execution-state-machine.json states array.

invariants.description: Destructive healing actions must never execute without a pre-action snapshot and explicit authorization.
invariants.remediation: Abort healing operation. Require --destructive flag and --no-snapshot=false. Validate via self-heal.ts scan before heal.

invariants.description: Runtime audit severity score must remain above 80/100 with zero critical findings for execution to be considered healthy.
invariants.remediation: Address all critical findings immediately. Resolve errors before next execution transition. Run audit-runtime.ts and follow remediation plan.

invariants.description: No registry entry may reference a file that does not exist on disk.
invariants.remediation: Run normalize-governance-registry.ts to detect orphans. Restore missing files or remove stale registry entries. Validate via registry normalization engine.

invariants.description: Every protocol definition must validate against protocol-definition.schema.json.
invariants.remediation: Fix schema violations in protocol JSON. Run AJV validation. Add missing required fields. Update schema if legitimate evolution.

invariants.description: Exactly one operational mode may be active at any time across the entire runtime.
invariants.remediation: Verify operational-mode.json registry. Check canonical-state.json and runtime-state.json for mode conflicts. Enter RECONCILE if multiple modes active.

invariants.description: Recovery modes automatically suspend all execution authority. No ticket advancement or lock acquisition permitted.
invariants.remediation: Verify execution-freeze.json guarantees are active. Check freeze_state in canonical-state. Block execution until freeze confirmed.

invariants.description: Recovery modes may only operate when execution freeze has been initiated and confirmed.
invariants.remediation: Initiate freeze protocol before entering recovery mode. Verify all freeze guarantees active.

invariants.description: No execution may be active while runtime is in FROZEN state.
invariants.remediation: Terminate or complete active execution before freezing. Verify active-execution.json shows null execution.

invariants.description: Thaw may only complete after all integrity validation criteria pass.
invariants.remediation: Run full integrity validation suite. Address all failing criteria before thaw authorization.

invariants.description: All drift events must be classified into exactly one category from the drift taxonomy.
invariants.remediation: Classify drift using drift-classification.json taxonomy. Emit standardized drift report.

invariants.description: Critical severity drift must trigger execution freeze before any repair action.
invariants.remediation: Immediately initiate freeze protocol. Enter RECONCILE mode. No repair without freeze.

invariants.description: Every mutation during recovery must have a corresponding repair contract with mutation log and audit event.
invariants.remediation: Create repair contract before executing mutation. Record exact old/new values. Emit governance event.

invariants.description: Repairs must be idempotent. Re-executing the same repair on already-repaired state must be a no-op.
invariants.remediation: Design repairs to check current state before mutation. Skip if target state already achieved.

invariants.description: Recovery may not be marked complete until all integrity validation exit criteria pass.
invariants.remediation: Run full integrity validation suite. Do not authorize thaw until all criteria pass.

invariants.description: Trust hierarchy must form a strict total order with no ambiguous rankings.
invariants.remediation: Verify authority-reconstruction.json trust hierarchy. Resolve any ambiguous rankings.

invariants.description: Append-only journal must never be mutated, truncated, or reordered.
invariants.remediation: Reject any operation that modifies existing journal entries. Journal corruption requires incident escalation.
